source_jobs.title: Investor Services Officer - Adjustments Team
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity:

Our Customer Operations team plays a critical role in delivering reliable, high-quality outcomes for Netwealth advisers and clients. Within Customer Operations, the Adjustments Team is responsible for some of the most complex and technical activities across our Payments function.

This is not a traditional administration role. We're looking for people who enjoy investigating issues, resolving exceptions, reconciling complex transactions, and working through scenarios that require analytical thinking and sound judgement.

You'll work across activities including contribution adjustments, recontribution strategies, asset transfers, reconciliation activities, and exception-based processing. Every day presents a different challenge, making this an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys getting to the root cause of issues and finding the best outcome.

Working in a high-volume, fast-paced environment, you'll build deep expertise across investment, superannuation and payments processes while contributing to exceptional outcomes for advisers and clients.

This role also offers strong career development opportunities. As your expertise grows, you'll have opportunities to expand your knowledge across broader Customer Operations functions, opening pathways into technical specialist, project and broader business roles.

In this role, you will:

 - Process and investigate complex investment, superannuation and payments transactions

 - Manage reconciliation activities, exceptions and adjustment-related casework

 - Analyse issues, identify root causes and determine appropriate resolutions

 - Manage a high-volume workload while maintaining strong quality standards

 - Work closely with internal stakeholders to resolve complex operational matters

 - Contribute to process improvements and operational efficiency initiatives

 - Build deep product, process and technical expertise across Customer Operations


ABOUT YOU

You're naturally curious, analytical and enjoy solving problems.

You don't just process tasks. You like understanding how things work, identifying discrepancies, and working through complex scenarios to reach the right outcome. You're comfortable spending time investigating issues, reconciling data, and making informed decisions based on the information available.

You thrive in environments where accuracy matters and enjoy work that requires both attention to detail and critical thinking.

You'll bring:

 - Experience in reconciliation, payments, operations, adjustments or administration within financial services

 - Exposure to superannuation, investments, payments, banking or another regulated environment

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills

 - Experience investigating exceptions, discrepancies or complex casework

 - High attention to detail and the ability to maintain accuracy under pressure

 - Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage competing priorities

 - Confidence making decisions and

source_jobs.title: General Manager - Custody
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity

Custody sits at the heart of Netwealth, a critical control point responsible for the safekeeping, settlement and reconciliation of more than $100B in client assets.

As General Manager – Custody, you’ll lead a high‑impact function that ensures every transaction is executed accurately, reconciled effectively, and compliant with regulatory obligations. You’ll oversee settlements, transfers and reconciliations, acting as the senior escalation point internally and with key external partners including ASX, Calastone, fund managers and registries.

This is a lean, high-performing team operating at significant scale. The role is less about hands-on operations and more about oversight, control, risk management and leadership. Ensuring the function runs with precision, discipline and resilience.

You’ll join at a pivotal time. The custody landscape is evolving rapidly, with change across CHESS replacement, digitisation, netting and AI-driven automation. We’re looking for someone who can bring fresh thinking, strengthen what works today, and help shape what Custody looks like next.

In this role, you will:

 - Lead end-to-end custody operations across a high-volume environment

 - Maintain strong controls across reconciliations, risk and compliance

 - Act as the key escalation point for complex internal and external issues

 - Build trusted relationships with external providers and industry bodies

 - Represent Custody in senior forums, including risk and compliance discussions

 - Drive process improvement, automation and operational efficiency

 - Lead and develop a small, experienced team

About you

You’re an experienced leader from custody, investment operations or a similarly high‑volume environment where accuracy, control and risk matter.

You combine strong technical depth with a practical leadership style. Someone who can oversee complex operations, improve processes and lead through change without overcomplicating things.

You’re calm under pressure, comfortable with competing priorities, and confident engaging with senior stakeholders and external providers. Importantly, you know how to lead lean teams, building trust, accountability and clarity.

You’ll bring:

 - Senior leadership experience in custody, operations, registries, broking or similar environments

 - Strong knowledge of CHESS, settlement processes and regulatory frameworks

 - Exposure to Calastone and managed fund operations (highly regarded)

 - Experience leading high-volume, high-risk operational teams

 - Strong relationship management across external partners and industry bodies

 - A track record of improving processes, strengthening controls and driving efficiency

 - A leadership style that empowers and develops others

 - An interest in industry change, including automation and AI

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to sup

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


THE OPPORTUNITY

Where curiosity meets impact.

At Netwealth, we're building the foundations that will shape the future of wealth technology. As we continue to invest in modern engineering practices, platform capability and AI-enabled ways of working, we're creating technology that is fast, reliable, secure and built to scale. Our focus is simple: get the foundations right so great ideas can move from concept to customer faster than ever before.

We're looking for a Full Stack Developer to join one of our product tribes and help build experiences relied on by thousands of advisers and investors across Australia. Working across both backend and frontend technologies, you'll develop modern solutions using C# .NET, Azure and Angular, collaborating closely with Product Owners, Quality Engineers and other Developers to deliver meaningful outcomes for customers.

This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys learning, asks thoughtful questions and is motivated by continuous improvement. You'll work alongside experienced engineers who are passionate about their craft, in an environment that encourages knowledge sharing, experimentation and growth.

You'll be trusted to contribute ideas, solve problems and take ownership of your work, while being supported by a team that genuinely wants to see you succeed. Whether you're refining your technical skills, exploring new technologies or broadening your understanding of product development, you'll have the opportunity to grow your career while working on technology that makes a real impact.

About You

You're an engineer who enjoys solving complex problems and finding simple, effective solutions. You care about building quality software, working collaboratively and continually improving both your skills and the products you help create.

You'll bring:

 - Expertise building server and client-side applications using C# .NET within complex enterprise environments.

 - A desire to learn, adapt and embrace new ideas and technologies

 - Knowledge of SQL Server and NoSQL databases

 - Experience working with Azure and cloud-based technologies

 - Exposure to Angular and/or React development
   
   Most importantly, you're someone who is naturally curious, enjoys working with smart and supportive people, and is excited by the opportunity to build meaningful products while developing your career in a modern, high-performing engineering environment.

And if you don't meet every requirement, we'd still love to hear from you. We know great developers come from a variety of backgrounds and experiences, and we're committed to creating an environment where people can learn, grow and do their best work.

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.



Roles:

 - 1 x Product Manager – Data Integration and Connectivity (permanent)

 - 1 x Product Manager – Advice Fees and Charging Architecture (permanent)

 - 1 x Product Manager – Investment Administration and Reporting (permanent)

 - 1 x Product Manager – Unified Orders (12 month fixed term contract)



The Opportunity

Netwealth is continuing to invest in building best-in-class platform capabilities and is looking for multiple Product Managers to join our growing product team.

These roles sit across key areas of the platform, from data and integrations through to fees, reporting, and investment administration. Each plays a critical role in how we deliver scalable, reliable, and high quality outcomes for advisers and clients.

Working in a product-led environment, you will own problems to solve rather than a backlog of features. You will be responsible for understanding how your domain works today, identifying where value can be created, and partnering with engineering and stakeholders to deliver meaningful improvements over time.

This is an opportunity to work on complex, high impact areas of the platform where the problems are real, the systems are deeply interconnected, and the outcomes matter.


Key Responsibilities

 - Taking ownership of a product domain and developing a deep understanding of how it operates, including underlying systems, data, and workflows

 - Identifying the most important problems to solve, balancing customer, operational, and business needs

 - Leading product discovery to understand pain points, test ideas, and shape the right solutions

 - Working closely with engineering to design and deliver improvements through small, iterative releases

 - Simplifying complex, process driven areas and reducing manual handling and risk

 - Using data and insights to inform decisions, measure outcomes, and continuously improve the product

 - Balancing BAU, regulatory, and operational priorities with longer term product and platform improvements

 - Partnering with stakeholders across technology, operations, finance, and risk to align on priorities and drive outcomes



About You

You’re a Product Manager who enjoys working in complex, data rich environments where the problems are not always obvious and the value comes from getting the fundamentals right.

You’re comfortable being given problems to solve rather than a list of features to deliver, and you work closely with your team to find the best path forward. You care about outcomes, use data to guide your thinking, and take a pragmatic approach to delivery.

Your experience includes:

 - Proven product management experience within investment platforms, financial services, or similarly complex environments

 - Strong product experience working with data, integrations, workflows, or operational systems is essential

 - Exposure to investment administration, tax reporting, client reporting, or asset management processes is ideal

 - Experience working with data heavy sys

source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Developer
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

We’re lucky to be part of a progressive, modern fintech that genuinely invests in tools that lift productivity, reduce friction, and help great engineers do great work.

You’ll join a supportive, cross-functional team of Developers, Product Owners, and Quality Engineers. Together, you’ll design and build server-side and client-side components using C#.NET, Microservices, and Azure but more importantly, you’ll help shape solutions that make a real difference.

You’ll collaborate closely with Developers, Product Owners, and Quality Engineers to design and build backend services and client-side components using C#.NET, Microservices, Angular and Azure. You’ll also work with stakeholders across the business to understand their needs and translate them into elegant, reliable solutions.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

You care about writing clean, maintainable code and solving problems with creativity and clarity. You’re someone who enjoys learning, sharing expertise and ideas, and building things that last.

Here’s what we’re hoping you’ll bring:

 - Experience building applications with C#.NET v8 with familiarity with MS SQL Server and NoSQL databases

 - Hands-on experience with Azure and cloud-based technologies

 - Knowledge, skills and motivations of Angular and/or React

 - A collaborative mindset and a genuine interest in stakeholder engagement

 - Personal attributes and self motivation to learn and share technical expertise.

 - Mentoring skills that allows you to provide value above your individual contribution.

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow here.

Our flexible hybrid model supports balance, with just 4 days in the office each fortnight. When you’re in, you’ll enjoy working from one of our modern, thoughtfully designed spaces across Australia – including our state-of-the-art Melbourne CBD office. All locations feature contemporary facilities to make your day comfortable, and in Melbourne, you’ll also enjoy perks like end-of-trip amenities and daily breakfast to make your commute easier.

We offer benefits designed for your wellbeing and personal development, including;

 - Family-friendly support: Paid parental leave and a fully funded school holiday program

 - Wellness perks: CU Health (virtual healthcare), income protection, flu shots, wellness weeks, retail discounts and financial wellbeing services

 - A vibrant culture: social events, trivia nights, and corporate sports

 - Employee Resource Groups: LGBTQIA+, DAWN (Development and Accelerating Women at Netwealth), Culture Group an

source_jobs.title: Head of Cyber Security
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


THE OPPORTUNITY

This is a rare opportunity to build and lead a newly elevated Cyber Security function at Netwealth.

Reporting directly to the CTO, you'll take ownership of our cyber security strategy and security posture end-to-end, spanning Security Operations, Application Security, AI Security Governance and our obligations under APRA CPS 234.

The team has built a strong foundation and the opportunity now is to broaden the remit from a traditional security operations focus to a modern, secure-by-design capability that enables the business to scale safely and confidently.

This is not a governance-heavy cyber role.

We're looking for a leader who can build security into systems, platforms and software delivery from the start. Someone who believes the secure path should be the easiest path, and who can embed guardrails, automation and secure defaults into the way engineers work every day.

You'll lead a high-performing team of approximately 20 people while helping shape how Netwealth responds to a rapidly changing threat landscape, including emerging technologies, AI adoption and evolving regulatory expectations.

In this role, you will:

 - Lead and grow Netwealth's Cyber Security function as a standalone capability

 - Own security operations, application security, AI security governance and security strategy

 - Drive a secure-by-design approach across software development and technology delivery

 - Build automated guardrails and security controls into platforms, pipelines and engineering workflows

 - Ensure Netwealth continues to meet its APRA CPS 234 obligations while strengthening overall security maturity

 - Develop Netwealth's approach to securing AI and emerging technologies

 - Partner with Product, Engineering, Platform & Reliability, Risk and executive stakeholders to make security an enabler of the business, not a blocker

About you

You're a cyber security leader with strong engineering roots.

You've built and operated security capabilities inside modern technology organisations and understand how to scale security through automation, secure architecture and great engineering practices, not simply through policy, governance and headcount.

You're equally comfortable discussing threat modelling, application security and DevSecOps with engineers as you are presenting security strategy and risk posture to executive and board audiences.

Importantly, you're an enabler. You see risks early, but you also know how to solve for them. You can balance security, usability and business outcomes, helping teams move quickly without compromising trust.

You'll bring:

 - Experience leading a cyber security function within a regulated or high-trust environment

 - Strong security engineering fundamentals and hands-on technical depth

 - Expertise across security operations, application security, cloud security and DevSecOps

 - Experience embedding security into software delivery, CI/CD pipelines and engineering practices

 - A strong un

source_jobs.title: National Sales Director - Private Wealth and Broking
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity:

Netwealth is making a deliberate, long-term investment in the broking channel as part of our broader Private Wealth and Private Banking strategy. The appointment of a National Sales Director – Private Wealth and Broking reflects our continuing investment to support Australia’s leading broking firms

In this newly created, senior leadership role, you will define and lead Netwealth’s sales strategy in this channel, shaping our go-to-market approach and ongoing strategy, and representing Netwealth at the most senior levels of the market. You’ll engage directly with C-suite leaders, while also spending meaningful time in-market with advisers and brokers across Victoria and New South Wales, building advocacy.

While Netwealth has a strong reputation and existing relationships, this channel is not yet fully established therefore this is a hunting role. You’ll build the book, open doors, and convert long-term strategic conversations into sustainable partnerships, supported by clear investment signals from Netwealth, including the launch of iHIN and our broader capability roadmap.

You’ll also play a critical internal role: acting as the bridge between market realities and Netwealth’s product, operations, and leadership teams. Your insights will directly influence how our broking proposition evolves over time. Success in the first 12 months will be measured through a combination of revenue, net flows, and the establishment of a high-quality book of business.

Please note: this role can be based in Sydney or Melbourne, with national responsibility and regular time spent in the field.

About you;

You are a senior commercial leader with deep experience in the broking ecosystem and a well-established national network. You understand how broking firms operate, commercially, culturally, and strategically. You bring the credibility and gravitas to operate confidently with executive leadership teams.

Importantly, you build trust, influence through insight, and work collaboratively. You know how to advocate strongly and you align naturally with a values-led organisation.

You will likely bring:

 - Deep, hands-on experience in the broking market, with a strong understanding of broker and adviser pain points

 - A proven network across broking firms and senior industry stakeholders, with the ability to open doors quickly

 - Strong commercial acumen, including experience navigating complex deal dynamics and long sales cycles at enterprise level

 - The ability to operate effectively both top-down (C-suite strategy conversations) and bottom-up (in-field engagement and advocacy)

 - Enough platform and technology understanding to credibly articulate how Netwealth fits, while being curious and capable of building deeper product knowledge at pace

 - An appetite to help shape what this channel becomes, contributing to product discovery, proposition refinement, and go-to-market evolution

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling c

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity:

Our Customer Operations team plays a critical role in delivering reliable, high-quality outcomes for Netwealth advisers and clients. Working across investment and superannuation administration, the team helps ensure every customer interaction is supported by operational excellence, accuracy and care.

As an Investor Services Officer, you'll be responsible for processing a range of investment and super administration activities, helping to deliver seamless experiences while meeting service, quality and compliance standards. https://netwealth820-my.sharepoint.com/personal/mhairi_netwealth_com_au/_layouts/15/Doc.aspx?sourcedoc=%7B30203F21-E1AC-4FE3-B067-EE64F27A3634%7D&file=POSITION%20DESCRIPTION%20-%20Investor%20Services%20Officer%20-%20July%202026.docx&action=default&mobileredirect=true

You'll join a collaborative and supportive team where you'll be encouraged to build your knowledge across Netwealth products, systems and processes, while developing valuable financial services expertise.

This is a great opportunity for someone looking to build a long-term career. As your experience grows, you'll have opportunities to develop across Administration and Payments, expand your knowledge into broader Customer Operations functions such as Onboarding, and explore future pathways into technical specialist, project, and broader business roles.

In this role, you will:

 - Process investment and superannuation administration activities accurately and efficiently

 - Manage a high-volume workload while meeting service and quality standards

 - Support advisers, clients and internal stakeholders to deliver positive outcomes

 - Investigate, resolve and escalate operational issues when required

 - Build knowledge across Netwealth products, systems and processes

 - Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives that enhance efficiency and customer experience


ABOUT YOU

You don't need years of financial services experience to be successful in this role. We're looking for people with the right mindset, strong attention to detail, and a genuine commitment to delivering great outcomes.

You thrive in a fast-paced environment, can manage competing priorities, and take pride in getting the details right. You're curious, eager to learn, and enjoy solving problems while working as part of a supportive team.

You'll bring:

 - Experience in administration, customer service, operations, processing, or a similar environment

 - Strong attention to detail and a commitment to quality

 - Excellent organisational skills and the ability to manage workload effectively

 - Strong communication and stakeholder management skills

 - Sound problem-solving skills and the ability to learn quickly

 - A positive, collaborative and customer-focused approach

Experience in financial services, superannuation, investments, banking, or another regulated environment is advantageous but not essential.

Life At Netwealth

source_jobs.title: Assistant Analyst - Asset Transfers
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity:

As an Assistant Analyst, you'll play a pivotal role in our Asset Transfers team to facilitate the smooth processing of transfer instructions. You will deal with questions about current and new asset transfer work items, and work with external stakeholders and advisers to initiate, validate and process paperwork and any other tasks that make the transfers process smoother.

You have:

 - Solid understanding of Domestic and International securities (including warrants, LICs, ETFs), bonds, managed funds, International assets, and other unlisted assets.

 - Sufficient knowledge of Capital Gains tax to be able to review the various forms of Cost Base information provided with transfers and determine what additional information is required.

 - WRAP platform experience (highly regarded).

 - Experience within the finance industry is desirable.

While prior experience in FinTech or Financial Services, with an understanding of transfer processes is preferred, we also believe that a proactive attitude, strong work ethic and willingness to learn are just as essential as technical expertise.

We are also open to candidates with transferable skills that include;

 - Strong communication: to interact with third parties, advisers, and internal stakeholders, providing clear and accurate information on the asset transfer process

 - Organisation: to manage multiple items, prioritise tasks and meet deadlines

 - Problem solving: to identify and resolve any issues or challenges during the transfer process, such as missing paperwork or discrepancies

 - Attention to detail: to thoroughly review forms, ensuring accuracy and addressing any errors or inconsistencies

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow here.

Our flexible hybrid model supports balance, with just 4 days in the office each fortnight. When you’re in, you’ll enjoy working from one of our modern, thoughtfully designed spaces across Australia – including our state-of-the-art Melbourne CBD office. All locations feature contemporary facilities to make your day comfortable, and in Melbourne, you’ll also enjoy perks like end-of-trip amenities and daily breakfast to make your commute easier.

We offer benefits designed for your wellbeing and personal development, including;

 - Family-friendly support: Paid parental leave and a fully funded school holiday program

 - Wellness perks: CU Health (virtual healthcare), income protection, flu shots, wellness weeks, retail discounts and financial wellbeing services

 - A vibrant culture: social events, trivia nights,

source_jobs.title: Private Wealth Support Consultant
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.



Looking to step into a role where service meets sophistication?

As our business continues to grow, we are seeking our newest team member!

At Netwealth, we’re redefining the private wealth experience, and we’re looking for a Private Wealth Support Consultant to be at the heart of it.

This is your opportunity to deliver concierge-style support to high-value clients and financial advisers, while building deep expertise in investment products and platform services.

This isn’t your typical support role. You’ll be the front-line expert, managing end-to-end client interactions across phone, chat and email, translating complex investment concepts into clear, human conversations.

You’ll take ownership, solve problems, and create seamless experiences.

If you love fast-paced environments and delivering standout service, you’ll thrive here.



What you'll be doing

 - Delivering high-touch support across phone, chat and email

 - Managing queries from start to finish

 - Simplifying complex investment and platform information for clients and advisers

 - Building deep product knowledge across Netwealth’s offering

 - Working cross-functionally with Sales, Operations and Product teams

 - Providing a polished, professional experience to high-net-worth clientele



What you'll bring to the team

 - Experience in a call centre or high-volume customer environment

 - Strong capability across phone and messaging channels

 - Confidence managing multiple conversations and priorities at once

 - Excellent communication skills, clear, concise, and customer-first

 - A knack for problem solving and a readiness to take ownership



We set you up for success from day one:

 - Structured induction and onboarding

 - Client-specific training to build product expertise

 - Shadowing with experienced team members

 - Ongoing development in partnership with Sales and internal stakeholders



You’ll be joining a highly collaborative Private Wealth Support team, growing to 7 consultants, all reporting into the Head of Private Wealth Support.

It’s a tight-knit, supportive environment with strong alignment across Customer Operations.

source_jobs.title: Early Resolution Officer
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.



The opportunity

Are you someone that values being genuine, collaborative and curious?
In this role you'll play a key part in resolving customer complaints early, fairly and efficiently.

You’ll take ownership of customer queries at the earliest stage, working to understand what’s happened and resolve matters, often within five days, before they escalate.

It’s fast-paced, sometimes challenging, but incredibly rewarding when you can turn a difficult situation into a positive experience.

You’ll spend your time speaking with customers, investigating issues with internal teams, and delivering outcomes that are fair, consistent and aligned with regulatory requirements.

Along the way, you’ll keep a close eye on patterns and trends, helping the business learn and continuously improve.

We do the right thing for our customers, support each other, and are always looking for better ways to work.



Why this role is a great next step for you

 - You’ll join a tight-knit team of six, with real support from your Team Leader and peers

 - Structured onboarding with buddy support, call shadowing and ongoing coaching

 - This is a role where you can see the impact of your work daily through better customer outcomes

 - Career pathways into Remediation, Risk & Compliance, Quality or leadership roles



This is a role where you’ll balance empathy with compliance, make decisions with sometimes incomplete information, and manage multiple matters at once, but with the backing of a supportive team and a business that values doing things properly.



What you'll bring

 - Experience in complaints handling, dispute resolution, remediation or customer advocacy

 - Exposure to superannuation or investment products

 - A background in financial services, superannuation or another regulated environment

 - Knowledge of ASIC complaint handling (RG 271), or the ability to quickly build this

 - Strong communication skills and confidence handling sensitive or complex conversations

 - A proactive, “can-do” mindset with sound judgement and attention to detail



Bonus points if you have

 - A knack for identifying root causes or improving processes

 - Experience supporting vulnerable customers with empathy and care



So, if you’re someone who stays calm under pressure, enjoys solving problems, and takes pride in doing the right thing with empathy and compliance front of mind, you’ll fit right in at Netwealth!



Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow here.

Our flexible hybrid model supports balance, with just 4 days in the office each for

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.



The opportunity

Some people are at their best when helping others through a challenging moment.

They stay calm when things get busy. They listen before they speak. They genuinely care about finding the right solution and creating a positive experience, even when a customer is frustrated, confused or simply needs a little extra support.



If that sounds like you, we'd love to hear from you!



At Netwealth, our Customer Service Representatives are often the first point of contact for advisers and clients.

They're trusted problem solvers, relationship builders and brand ambassadors who play a critical role in delivering the exceptional service experience Netwealth is known for.

This role supports advisers and clients across phone, email and online chat, helping them navigate our platform, products and services while providing timely and accurate solutions.



We're looking for someone who combines resilience with empathy.



Someone who can confidently manage high volumes of enquiries, adapt to changing priorities and remain calm under pressure, while never losing sight of the person on the other end of the conversation.

You'll thrive in this role if you:

 - Genuinely enjoy helping people and finding solutions

 - Stay composed and professional during challenging customer interactions

 - Have a warm, approachable and positive communication style

 - Listen actively and ask thoughtful questions to understand customer needs

 - Can balance empathy with efficiency

 - Are curious, eager to learn and excited to build product knowledge

 - Take ownership, follow through on commitments and work well as part of a team

 - Bring a growth mindset and see challenges as opportunities to learn and improve



What you'll be doing

As part of the team, you'll:

 - Support advisers and clients via phone, email and online chat

 - Resolve product, platform and service enquiries

 - Investigate issues and provide practical solutions

 - Build strong knowledge of Netwealth's products and services

 - Work closely with teams across Operations, Sales, Product, Technology and Risk & Compliance

 - Maintain accurate records and ensure high-quality customer outcomes

 - Contribute to continuous improvement and help make great processes even better



What you'll bring

 - Previous customer service experience (financial services/platform experience is highly regarded)

 - Exceptional verbal and written communication skills

 - Strong problem-solving abilities and attention to detail

 - The ability to remain calm and solution-focused in demanding situations

 - Strong organisational skills and the ability to manage competing priorities

 - Experience in financial services, wealth management, superannuation or investments will be highly regarded

 - RG146 qualification (or willingness to obtain) is advantageous



Ready to make a difference?

source_jobs.title: Workday - Finance Data and Reporting Specialist- Fixed Term to April 2027
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The opportunity

If you enjoy turning complex data into meaningful insights, have a keen eye for detail and love solving problems, you'll thrive in this role. You'll play a key part in ensuring our finance data, reporting and testing activities are delivered with confidence as we move towards go-live.

This is the kind of role where your work will be highly visible, genuinely valued and critical to the success of a major business transformation.


WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

You'll work closely with Finance Process Owners, Workday consultants and project teams to:

 - Validate and reconcile finance data across general ledger, accounts payable, accounts receivable, fixed assets and fund accounting.

 - Investigate and resolve data issues, ensuring accuracy throughout the migration process.

 - Test and validate reporting outputs, identifying gaps and inconsistencies.

 - Support user acceptance testing (UAT) by preparing test data and assisting finance users through testing cycles.

 - Document finance processes, data definitions and workshop outcomes.

 - Help bridge the gap between finance, technology and the broader project team.

About you

You'll bring experience working with finance systems and data, ideally within a Workday Finance environment. You're naturally curious, analytical and enjoy getting into the detail while keeping the bigger picture in mind.

You'll likely have:

 - Experience with Workday Finance in corporate or fund accounting environments.

 - Strong knowledge of finance data structures and reconciliation processes.

 - Experience supporting system implementations or transformation projects.

 - Excellent analytical, communication and stakeholder management skills.

 - A collaborative approach and the confidence to work independently in a fast-moving environment.

Experience with Workday Prism Analytics, Accounting Centre, ERP testing or wealth management will be highly regarded.

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow here.

Our flexible hybrid model supports balance, with just 4 days in the office each fortnight. When you’re in, you’ll enjoy working from one of our modern, thoughtfully designed spaces across Australia – including our state-of-the-art Melbourne CBD office. All locations feature contemporary facilities to make your day comfortable, and in Melbourne, you’ll also enjoy perks like end-of-trip amenities and daily breakfast to make your commute easier.

We offer benefits designed for your wellbeing and personal development, including;

 - Family-friendly support: Paid parental leave and

source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity

This is a newly created role within our National Key Accounts team, a high-performing group responsible for some of Netwealth’s most important relationships.

Reporting to the National Head of Key Accounts, you’ll partner with large, complex licensee groups, operating at head office level to drive engagement, platform adoption and sustained net flow growth.

This isn’t a traditional sales role. It’s about influence. Setting clear account strategies, identifying growth opportunities, and aligning internal teams to deliver consistent outcomes at scale.

You’ll act as the connector across Sales, Marketing, Product and Operations, enabling teams, removing barriers to adoption, and strengthening Netwealth’s position across adviser networks.

Note: the role can be based in Sydney or Melbourne

In this role, you will:

 - Own and grow a portfolio of strategic licensee relationships

 - Build trusted, multi-level relationships across head office and adviser networks

 - Develop and execute structured account plans aligned to growth targets

 - Drive platform adoption, engagement and net flows

 - Identify and unlock opportunities while removing barriers to growth

 - Align internal teams to deliver coordinated, scalable outcomes

 - Enable BDMs and TRMs with insights and direction to drive adviser activity

 - Deliver strategic outcomes including partnerships, APL inclusions and key initiatives

About You

You’re a relationship and growth leader who knows how to influence outcomes in complex environments.

You’re comfortable operating at head office level, building trust with senior stakeholders while balancing strategy with execution. You bring a strong commercial lens, using data and insight to shape plans and drive results.

Importantly, you know how to lead without authority, aligning teams, creating momentum, and delivering through others.

You’ll bring:

 - Experience in key account management, platform sales or relationship roles within financial services

 - Strong understanding of the financial advice and licensee landscape

 - Knowledge of platforms, managed accounts and investment solutions

 - Proven experience managing large, strategic client relationships

 - Exceptional stakeholder management and influencing skills

 - Strong commercial acumen and strategic thinking

 - Ability to operate across both strategy and execution

 - A data-led mindset, translating insights into action

 - A collaborative approach and ability to lead without direct authority

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity:

Join one of the largest Sales & Distribution teams in the Australian platform market and play a key role in building out our broking channel.

This is a growth role, focused on driving new business, strengthening client relationships, and expanding Netwealth’s presence across advisers, brokers and dealer groups.

As a Distribution Manager, Victoria you’ll build pipeline from the ground up while also developing an existing client base. Success comes from strong market engagement, disciplined execution, and the ability to position a compelling case for change in a competitive landscape.

You’ll partner closely with senior stakeholders to drive activity, progress opportunities, and deliver against revenue targets.

In this role, you will:

 - Build pipeline and convert new business opportunities

 - Grow and support an existing client base

 - Develop strong relationships across advisers, brokers and dealer groups

 - Represent Netwealth in market and at industry events

 - Progress opportunities from initial engagement to conversion

 - Contribute market insights to support product and go-to-market strategy

About you;

You’re a commercially driven sales professional who thrives in a fast-paced, growth environment.

You’re confident building relationships, creating opportunities, and following through to close. You bring enough technical understanding to be credible, but your strength is in how you engage, influence and execute.

You’re proactive, resilient, and take ownership of your pipeline and outcomes.

You’ll bring:

 - 2–4 years’ experience in sales, distribution or business development across Broking

 - Strong understanding of wealth management and platforms

 - Proven ability to build pipeline and win new business

 - Strong communication, influencing and presentation skills

 - A commercial mindset and ability to position against competitors

 - RG146 and willingness to travel interstate

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

source_jobs.title: Assistant Analyst - Operations Governance
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.



As our team continues to grow, we are seeking an Assistant Analyst to join us.

If you’re someone who loves connecting the dots, asking “why?”, and spotting what others might miss, this could be your next move.

You’ll join our Operations Governance team, where the work sits at the heart of risk, compliance and customer outcomes, protecting both our clients and the business while continuously improving how we operate.

At Netwealth, Customer Operations is built around delivering exceptional outcomes across the entire customer lifecycle, from onboarding and transactions through to complaints, remediation and risk management.

This role plays a key part in making sure we do that in a way that is not only efficient, but compliant and thoughtful.



What you'll be doing

You’ll work closely with Analysts and your Team Leader to support day-to-day governance activities, getting exposure to a really interesting mix of transaction monitoring, investigations, reporting and process improvement.

Your day to day will look like:

 - Reviewing transaction alerts and identifying unusual or suspicious activity

 - Investigating customer behaviours and transaction patterns to assess potential risk

 - Supporting case management, tracking and reporting across the team

 - Contributing to monthly reporting by pulling together data, insights and trends

 - Working with internal stakeholders to gather information and support investigations

 - Ensuring compliance incidents are captured and managed in a timely way

 - Keeping across regulatory changes and supporting the business in preparing for what’s ahead

 - Identifying opportunities to improve processes and make things more efficient

You’ll also get exposure across multiple parts of the business, collaborating with teams like Sales, Product, IT and Risk & Compliance.



What you'll bring

You don’t need to know everything on day one, but you will bring a strong foundation and a genuine interest in learning.

Must-have:

 - 1–2 years’ experience in administration, operations or a similar role

 - Strong organisation skills and the ability to manage competing priorities

 - A naturally curious mindset with attention to detail

 - Confidence working with data, systems or reporting tools

 - Clear communication and the ability to investigate and document findings effectively

Nice to have:

 - Exposure to financial services, or a regulated environment

 - Experience or interest in financial crime, fraud, scams or compliance



This isn’t just about processing tasks, it’s about building capability. You’ll develop a strong foundation in AML/CTF, transaction monitoring and operational governance, while working alongside a collaborative team who genuinely support each other and share knowledge.



You’ll also be part of a team that:

 - Values curiosity and continuous improvement

 - Encourages you to ask questions and challenge assumptions

 - Supports your development through coaching and on-the-job learning

source_jobs.title: Senior Legal Counsel
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


THE OPPORTUNITY



At Netwealth, growth has never been just about scale. It has been about evolution and doing things better. As the business continues to expand, so too does the need for a legal function that is not only highly capable, but forward thinking and embedded in the way the organisation operates.

This is where the Senior Legal Counsel role comes in.

This is a newly created opportunity, designed for someone who is excited by the idea of shaping something meaningful. You will play a central role in evolving the legal function into a contemporary, best practice in house team. Along the way, you will partner closely with Boards, Committees, Executive teams and stakeholders across the business, offering trusted advice that enables confident, well informed decision making.

It is a role that goes beyond day to day legal work. You will be involved in a broad range of interesting and complex matters, build strong relationships across Netwealth, and contribute to the direction and maturity of the legal function as a whole.




ABOUT YOU

We are looking for someone who brings both depth of expertise and a genuine appetite to make an impact.

You are a qualified lawyer, admitted as a Barrister and Solicitor with a current practising certificate. With over five years of experience in financial services law, whether in house or in private practice, you have developed a strong technical foundation and a clear understanding of what best practice legal support looks like within an organisation.

More than that, you are someone who is comfortable navigating change and contributing to it. You enjoy finding better ways of doing things and can balance strategic thinking with hands on delivery.

Your experience tells a broader story:

 - You have worked within wealth or financial services, with exposure to managed investments, IDPS or superannuation highly valued

 - You bring a strong understanding of financial services regulation, including the Corporations Act, Superannuation Industry Supervision Act and APRA Prudential Standards

 - You are confident drafting and negotiating a range of commercial agreements, including distribution and platform arrangements

 - You have supported product governance, disclosure documentation and regulatory change initiatives

 - You are able to step between big picture thinking and managing individual matters with care and precision

 - You are a clear communicator who can translate complex legal concepts into practical guidance. You build trust with stakeholders, manage your time well, and are comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative, fast moving environment.

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Developme

Our Training & Relationship Managers (TRMs) are the heartbeat of our adviser experience.

They’re the trusted partners, the problem-solvers, the ones who make our clients feel supported every step of the way.

Now, we’re looking for a Team Leader to take this to the next level.

This isn’t just about managing a team, it’s about shaping how we show up to the market!

You’ll lead, coach and elevate a group of high-performing TRMs, helping them deliver standout service, deepen adviser relationships and ultimately drive growth across the region.

You’ll sit at the intersection of leadership, strategy and relationship management, partnering closely with our Sales Leadership Group, influencing direction, and playing a key role in strengthening Netwealth’s position in the market.

You balance commercial thinking with relationship depth, and you’re just as comfortable talking strategy as you are jumping in to help solve a tricky client issue.

You’re:

 - Customer-first, always

 - Collaborative and generous with your knowledge

 - Agile and comfortable navigating change

 - Curious, proactive and always looking for better ways of doing things

 - Results-driven, but never at the expense of relationships
