source_jobs.title: Software Engineer in Test
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


SOFTWARE ENGINEER IN TEST

We're transforming how technology teams build, test and deliver software. We're creating an engineering culture built on ownership, automation and continuous improvement, where quality is embedded from day one, not tested at the end.

As a Software Engineer in Test, you'll help shape that future.

Working within a cross-functional delivery team, you'll build automated testing solutions, improve engineering practices and help teams release software with greater speed and confidence.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Design and develop automated testing frameworks and solutions driving quality engineering and shift-left testing practices

 - Improve test coverage across API, integration and UI layers

 - Integrate testing into CI/CD pipelines

 - Partner with Software Engineers, Product Owners and stakeholders to identify and manage quality risks

 - Contribute to exploratory testing, release verification and continuous improvement initiatives

 - Help create faster feedback loops and more reliable releases


WHAT YOU'LL BRING

 - Strong development skills in C#, .NET, TypeScript, JavaScript or SQL Server

 - Expertise in building and maintaining test automation frameworks

 - Expertise with API, integration and UI automation

 - Knowledge of CI/CD and DevOps practices

 - A passion for automation, quality and continuous improvement

 - A collaborative mindset and willingness to challenge the status quo


WHY NETWEALTH?

We're investing heavily in our technology future and creating an environment where engineers can do their best work.

You'll benefit from:

 - Real ownership – the teams building our products have the authority to make technical decisions and own outcomes end-to-end.

 - Engineering-led quality – we're moving beyond traditional QA models, with quality built into how software is designed and delivered.

 - Technical career progression – grow through a technical craft path or people leadership path, with equal opportunity, recognition and reward.

 - AI-enabled ways of working – we're investing in the platforms, tooling and foundations that help engineers work smarter.

 - A growing technology organisation – this is a period of investment, growth and transformation across Engineering, Platform, Reliability, Cyber Security and Data & AI.

 - Modern engineering practices – automation, observability, reliability and continuous delivery are at the heart of how we work.

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

We're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's top 5 companies for Career Development (endorsed by WORK180). From further education and diverse training opportunities to strong support for internal mobility. Your career can grow here.

Our flexible hybrid model supports balance, with just 4 days in the of

source_jobs.title: General Manager - Platform, Reliability and Quality
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Opportunity

This is one of the most important technology leadership roles at Netwealth.

We're creating a brand-new Platform, Reliability and Quality function that brings together Cloud & Infrastructure, Site Reliability Engineering, Developer Platform, Quality Engineering, Environment Management and Release Management under one leader. Your mission is simple but ambitious: build the platform, tooling and automation that enable our engineers to deliver more value, faster and with greater confidence.

Reporting to the CTO, you'll lead a major transformation in how software is built and run at Netwealth. You'll treat the platform as a product, with engineers as your customers, creating a self-service engineering experience that embeds quality, accelerates delivery and makes the secure path the easy path.

This isn't a traditional infrastructure role or a cloud migration program. It's an opportunity to build a capability we don't have today. One that fundamentally changes engineering productivity and reliability across the organisation.

In this role, you will:

 - Establish and lead the new Platform, Reliability and Quality function, bringing three teams together under a single operating model

 - Define and execute the strategy across cloud, infrastructure, SRE, CI/CD, developer experience and quality engineering

 - Build an internal developer platform that enables teams to self-serve and move faster

 - Drive automation, standardisation and engineering efficiency across Technology

 - Lead secure-by-default and quality-by-default engineering practices

 - Own platform reliability, cloud economics and operational resilience

 - Build and develop a high-performing team of engineering leaders and specialists

Success in this role means creating an environment where the journey from code complete to production becomes measured in hours, not days or weeks.

About You

You're a technology leader who has built platforms, not just operated them.

You understand that high-performing engineering organisations scale through automation, tooling, developer experience and modern ways of working, not simply by adding more engineers. You know how to create the conditions that enable teams to move quickly without compromising reliability, security or quality.

You've led large technology functions, delivered meaningful transformation, and successfully influenced senior stakeholders across engineering and business teams. You're equally comfortable discussing platform architecture, coaching leaders, managing budgets and driving strategic change.

You'll bring:

 - Experience leading platform, infrastructure, cloud, reliability or engineering enablement functions

 - Proven success as a leader of leaders, delivering outcomes through high-performing teams

 - Deep expertise across cloud, infrastructure, containers, networking, CI/CD and developer tooling

 - Strong understanding of SRE, observability, resilience and platform operations at scale

 - Experienc

source_jobs.title: Remediation Officer
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The opportunity

Sometimes, things don’t go to plan.. A process misfires, a transaction doesn’t land quite right, or a customer experience falls short of what we pride ourselves on.

At Netwealth, our Remediation team sits at the heart of Customer Operations, bringing together service delivery, risk, complaints, and operational expertise to ensure we deliver the right outcomes for our customers, every time.

We’re looking for a Remediation Officer to join a close-knit team and play a key role in identifying, investigating and resolving errors while keeping customers informed, supported and confident along the way.

If you’re someone who thrives in a fast-moving environment where detail matters and no two days look the same, we’re ready to welcome someone who can hit the ground running and make an immediate impact.

 

What you'll be doing

You’ll be the person who turns complexity into clarity. The one who takes ownership, prioritises effectively, and ensures customers are never left in the dark.

In this role, you will:

 - Manage your own queue and prioritise cases based on risk and deadlines

 - Investigate and resolve remediation cases with accuracy and care

 - Communicate clearly and confidently with customers—both written and over the phone

 - Work closely with internal teams across operations, risk and product

 - Identify recurring issues and contribute to ongoing process improvement

 - Stay across evolving products, processes and regulatory requirements

This role is about resolving issues efficiently while delivering a seamless customer experience and avoiding complaints wherever possible.

 

What you'll bring

 - Experience in financial services (super, investments, wrap platforms or insurance)

 - Strong attention to detail and a commitment to delivering quality outcomes

 - Excellent communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex information

 - A genuine customer-first mindset and passion for service excellence

 - The ability to juggle competing priorities while maintaining accuracy

 - Confidence working both independently and collaboratively

 

Nice to have:

 - Experience in remediation, complaints or operational processing

 - Familiarity with regulatory frameworks

 - Exposure to process improvement or change initiatives




This isn’t just about fixing errors, it’s about learning, growing and building expertise that opens doors across Netwealth.

 

You’ll benefit from:

 - Structured onboarding across our products, processes and workflows

 - Ongoing training and continuous learning

 - Close guidance from an experienced Team Leader

 - A collaborative team environment where knowledge is shared

 - Exposure to increasingly complex cases as your capability grows

 

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

W

source_jobs.title: Investment Analyst
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.

The Team

The Investment Solutions and Governance team at Netwealth is responsible for overseeing the research and governance of investment options available on the Netwealth investment menu.

Netwealth’s product suite includes Superannuation and IDPS platforms, Managed Accounts, and Global Specialist Series funds. Members of the Investment Management & Research (IMR) team work closely with the broader Product Department to manage the investments offered to clients on the Superannuation and IDPS investment menus and products issued by Netwealth as responsible entity.

The Opportunity
We are seeking an experienced Investment Analyst (or Manager-level researcher) with a specialisation in alternative strategies and complex investment products. The ideal candidate will have approximately five years of relevant experience (minimum three years).

As an Investment Analyst within the IMR team, you will report to the Head of Investment Research and Governance and play a key role in supporting Netwealth’s investment governance framework.

This role focuses on alternative strategies and complex investment products, including private markets, hedge funds, structured products, and other non-traditional solutions. You will support the selection, due diligence, and ongoing governance of specialist managers and products in these areas.

Key Responsibilities

 - Support the review, selection, and ongoing monitoring of alternative strategies and complex investment products

 - Conduct detailed due diligence on external fund managers, including meetings and qualitative assessments in adherence to the Investment Governance Framework/s

 - Prepare high-quality research, investment papers, and recommendations for Investment Committees

 - Ongoing governance of the investment menu, including investment option addition, monitoring, and removals

 - Support Investment Committee reporting and broader IMR work plan initiatives

About You
You are an intellectually curious investment professional with a strong interest in alternative strategies and complex product structures. You may come from a research team within a research house, investment consulting firm, dealer group, wealth management firm, or platform environment.

You enjoy analysing how products are constructed, understanding where risks lie, and assessing performance across different market conditions. You are comfortable engaging with fund managers and forming independent views.

You also bring strong communication skills and can translate complex concepts into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.

Your Experience

 - Tertiary qualification in finance, commerce, or a related discipline; CFA or equivalent is highly regarded

 - Experience researching or analysing alternative investments (e.g. private markets, hedge funds, structured products)

 - Understanding of a Responsible Entity for a registered Managed Investment Schemes and Super Trustee regulations (SPS530)

 - Strong understanding of investmen

source_jobs.title: Head of Investment Menu Product
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.





THE OPPORTUNITY - SYDNEY OR MELBOURNE BASED

Netwealth’s Platform Product function is responsible for the strategic development, maintenance and continuous innovation of our flagship wrap platforms, Super Accelerator and Wealth Accelerator.

As Head of Investment Menu Products, you will lead the governance, commercialisation and DDO framework across our investment menus and Netwealth-issued managed investment schemes. This is a high-impact leadership role, pivotal to strengthening our investment product operating model, maintaining clear operational independence, and ensuring our platform continues to deliver industry-leading outcomes in an increasingly regulated environment.

Key responsibilities:

 - Lead the end‑to‑end investment product operating model, including governance frameworks, investment onboarding, in‑life product activities, and maintaining clear operational independence while partnering with Investment Governance & Research.

 - Drive execution and continuous uplift of the DDO framework across all investment menus and managed investment schemes, ensuring strong distribution oversight, monitoring, review triggers, and alignment with Legal, Risk & Compliance, the Trustee Office and Operations.

 - Lead commercial negotiations with fund managers to secure sustainable, member‑benefit–aligned arrangements.

 - Coordinate a complex stakeholder ecosystem across Product, Investments, Operations, Distribution, Legal, Risk & Compliance, Technology and external managers, providing accurate governance reporting and proactive risk management to senior leadership and committees.

 - Support strategic growth through disciplined investment menu expansion, prioritisation and commercial execution, ensuring competitive differentiation while maintaining strong governance and member‑outcomes focus.




ABOUT YOU

You’re a strategic, outcomes-driven leader with the credibility to operate at senior levels and the discipline to build scalable governance in a regulated environment. You bring strong judgement, influence and the ability to balance compliance requirements with commercial outcomes.

 - 8–12 years’ experience in investment product governance, platform product, superannuation, or related financial services.

 - Strong understanding of APRA prudential standards, ASIC (DDO), and superannuation legislation.

 - Demonstrated capability in process design, governance uplift, and regulatory operating model implementation.

 - Experience managing complex conflicts of interest and navigating commercial arrangements.

 - Strong stakeholder influence, communication and executive reporting capabilities.

 - Values-led leader with experience building high-performing teams and fostering governance excellence.



Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life because when you thrive, so do we.

source_jobs.title: Procurement Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Netwealth

Netwealth isn't just a company. We're shaping the future of wealth in Australia. Our award-winning platform and NextGen technology empower advisers and investors to achieve more, and we're proud to be recognised as one of Australia's most innovative FinTech businesses.

Since 1999, we've grown rapidly by challenging the status quo. We move fast, think big, and stay agile. Free from unnecessary bureaucracy so we can deliver smarter solutions for our clients and create real impact.

But what truly sets us apart? Our people. We're a team of curious, optimistic and courageous individuals who collaborate to make life better for more Australians. We value authenticity, and agility, and we believe in creating an environment where you can do your best work, grow your career, and feel part of something meaningful.

If you're looking for a place where your ideas matter, where innovation is celebrated, and where you can help shape a brighter financial future, join us at Netwealth.


THE OPPORTUNITY


Procurement at Netwealth is continuing to evolve, with a clear strategy in place and a focus on building a more mature, consistent and embedded function across the business.

As Procurement Manager, you will play a key role in progressing this next phase. You will bring structure, oversight and commercial discipline to how suppliers are assessed, onboarded and managed, ensuring procurement supports both business outcomes and regulatory requirements.

Working closely with Finance, Risk and Compliance, Legal and business stakeholders, you will help embed procurement earlier in the decision making process. This includes strengthening supplier governance, improving visibility across the supplier base and ensuring appropriate risk frameworks are in place, particularly in areas such as data protection, KYC and AML considerations.

With a large and diverse supplier base, including service providers subject to regulatory expectations, you will lead the uplift of supplier risk assessment and ongoing monitoring.

At the same time, you will continue to build procurement as a strategic function, balancing cost, risk and value to support better long term outcomes.



Your role will include:

 - Leading the evolution of the procurement strategy and operating model

 - Leading sourcing strategy, market assessments and tender processes across key supplier contracts

 - Partnering with stakeholders on supplier selection, business cases and commercial negotiations to deliver value outcomes

 - Conducting supplier due diligence and overseeing vendor risk management, including CPS 230 requirements

 - Building and embedding procurement frameworks aligned to APRA and broader regulatory standards

 - Owning supplier management end to end, including the register, performance monitoring and contract lifecycle

 - Driving commercial, risk aware decision making and delivering clear reporting across governance forums




ABOUT YOU

You are someone who sees procurement for what it should be. A strategic lever, not a back office process.

Your experience includes:

 - 7 to 10 years’ experience in procurement, ideally at a manager level with people leadership skills

 - Experience within procurement, ideally in a financial services or regulated environment

 - Strong understanding of supplier risk, governance and regulatory expectations

 - Experience across digital, IT or software procurement

 - Exposure to APRA or ASIC regulated environments

 - Strong attention to detail and well developed stakeholder engagement skills

 - Confidence partnering with senior stakeholders and contributing to commercial decision making

 - The ability to balance strategic thinking with hands on delivery

 - Experience building or uplifting procurement functions
   

Life At Netwealth

At Netwealth, we believe a fulfilling career is built on growth, balance, and belonging. Our people are our greatest strength, and we’re committed to supporting you at every stage of life b

notes: Netwealth is a FinTech company with a strong focus on wealth management and investment. The job postings suggest a mix of technical and non-technical roles, with a slight emphasis on operations and customer-facing positions.
